duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Relayer gas griefing and fee theft in processMessage: no gasleft() >= gasLimit check before forwarding to excessivelySafeCall, allowing relayers to provide insufficient gas, cause OOG failure, collect fee while message becomes RETRIABLE
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 10 threats describe the identical vulnerability: Bridge.processMessage passes _message.gasLimit to excessivelySafeCall without checking gasleft() is sufficient, enabling relayers to under-gas the call, cause OOG failure, and collect the fee. ECON-H001 selected as best representative due to most comprehensive attack economics and clear precondition analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Permanent message DoS via push-over-pull ETH refund pattern: reverting refundTo/srcOwner/destOwner contracts cause processMessage or recallMessage to revert entirely, permanently locking assets
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 9 threats describe the same push-over-pull DoS pattern where sendEther reverts if the recipient contract cannot accept ETH, permanently blocking message lifecycle progression. ECON-H011 selected as it covers the broadest scope (Bridge processMessage + all vault onMessageRecalled paths) with detailed impact assessment.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: changeBridgedToken deletes wrong mapping key: executes delete bridgedToCanonical[_btokenNew] (no-op) instead of delete bridgedToCanonical[btokenOld_], leaving stale canonical mapping
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 7 threats describe the identical code bug at ERC20Vault.changeBridgedToken where the wrong variable is used in the delete statement. TECH2-H003 here refers to the instance titled 'Stale Canonical Mapping Persists Due to Incorrect Deletion'. TECH-H003 selected for precise line references and clear technical description.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Short calldata (<4 bytes) bypasses onMessageInvocation selector check in _invokeMessageCall, allowing arbitrary fallback function invocation on target contracts
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 5 threats describe the same bypass: _message.data.length >= 4 check in _invokeMessageCall allows 1-3 byte payloads to skip selector enforcement and invoke target fallback functions. TECH2-H004 here refers to the instance titled 'Bypass of Selector Validation for Short Calldata'. TECH-H004 selected for most detailed technical analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Fee-on-transfer token accounting mismatch: source chain correctly measures balance delta, but destination chain _transferTokens uses safeTransfer with full amount, causing systematic vault underfunding
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 5 threats describe the identical asymmetry between source-side balance delta measurement and destination-side full-amount safeTransfer for fee-on-transfer tokens. ECON-H005 selected for comprehensive attack economics and impact analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: SignalService permanently unpausable: _authorizePause always reverts with SS_UNSUPPORTED, preventing emergency halt of proof verification
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 3 threats describe the same design issue: SignalService._authorizePause reverts, making pause/unpause inoperative with no emergency stop for proof verification. TECH-H006 selected for most detailed investigation rationale.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Unchecked addition overflow in processMessage: unchecked { invocationDelay += invocationExtraDelay } could wrap to small value if getInvocationDelays is overridden with large values in derived contracts
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 3 threats describe the identical unchecked arithmetic overflow risk on security-critical delay values in a virtual function context. TECH-H007 selected for clearest precondition analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Unauthorized chain data caching via proveSignalReceived: _cacheChainData path bypasses isAuthorized check required by public syncChainData, allowing permissionless cache writes
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the same authorization bypass: proveSignalReceived → _cacheChainData → _syncChainData writes chain data without isAuthorized check. TECH-H010 selected for more comprehensive function chain analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Permanent token loss for banned addresses: messages to banned targets marked DONE without executing vault callbacks, preventing recall of tokens locked on source chain
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 3 threats describe the same issue: addressBanned check marks messages DONE without invoking payload, preventing recall. TECH2-H005 here refers to the instance titled 'Permanent Loss of Bridged Tokens for Banned Addresses'. ECON-H015 selected for most detailed economic impact assessment.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: BridgedERC20Base burn-before-mint ordering during migration: burn() calls mint on migratingAddress before _burnToken, creating temporary double supply and cross-contract reentrancy risk
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 3 threats describe the same mint-before-burn ordering issue in BridgedERC20Base.burn during outbound migration. TECH2-H006 here refers to the instance titled 'Cross-Contract Reentrancy Risk in Token Migration'. ECON2-H010 selected for most comprehensive analysis covering voting power and checkpoint implications.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: USDCAdapter approval mismatch: _burnToken uses usdc.transferFrom requiring direct user approval to adapter contract, breaking standard vault approval workflow
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the same approval flow mismatch: users approve ERC20Vault but USDCAdapter._burnToken needs direct approval to the adapter. ECON-H014 selected for more detailed description.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: retryMessage gas griefing: anyone can call retryMessage with insufficient gas for non-lastAttempt retries, keeping message in RETRIABLE state and griefing destOwner
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe retryMessage gas griefing where any caller submits low-gas retries to keep messages in RETRIABLE without fee penalty. TECH2-H004 here refers to the instance titled 'Relayer fee theft via deliberate under-gassing in Bridge.retryMessage'. ECON2-H008 selected for more detailed economic analysis and precondition specification.
